ogTitle: Innamincka vs Iron Knob: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Innamincka and Iron Knob.
heading: Innamincka vs Iron Knob — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Innamincka, South Australia and Iron Knob, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Iron Knob has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Innamincka (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Innamincka or Iron Knob safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Innamincka has a flood risk score of 21/100, while Iron Knob scores 20/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Innamincka scores 61/100 for bushfire risk. Iron Knob scores 42/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Innamincka or Iron Knob?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Iron Knob has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
